description_long: This is a rustic, double-handled vase with a wide, flared mouth and a rounded, bulbous body. The surface appears rough and uneven, with a weathered, matte finish that suggests it is made from coarse, unglazed ceramic or clay. The handles are thick and angular, attached symmetrically on opposite sides, providing both functional grip and visual balance. The coloration is predominantly earthy brown with subtle yellowish undertones, giving it an aged, natural look. The interior is visible at the mouth, indicating it is hollow and capable of holding contents such as water or flowers. The overall form and texture evoke traditional pottery often found in outdoor urban parks or historical public spaces.
description: A weathered, double-handled clay vase with a wide mouth and rough, earthy brown surface. Its rustic texture and robust form suggest durability and traditional craftsmanship.
description_view_0: Front view showing both angular handles and the wide, flared mouth with the bulbous body clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one handle in profile and the curvature of the vase's body, with the mouth and base aligned vertically.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying both handles and the rounded body, but with subtle differences in surface texture.
description_view_3: Opposite side view to view 1, showing the other handle in profile and the same vertical alignment of mouth and base.
